A0075.name: Access Instructions
A0075.description: Additional instructions necessary to support an agents visit.Examples: 'through the gate to left of front door', 'down unmarked lane beside the P.O'.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0101.name: Access Password
A0101.description: The password required to gain access to the persons property

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.
Any within the constraints of format.

A0003.name: Address Text
A0003.description: 'Postally not required' text such as a textual description of locational aspects not contained elsewhere. Example: Gas Beacon in field.
A0003.notes: There is no equivalent field on the S70, it has been introduced to be in line with the MPAS Metering Point Address Line 1 which is a 50 character field.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0102.name: Address Type Code
A0102.description: Code which defines the type of address usage.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General
As defined within Market Domain Data General
Any within the constraints of format.

A0139.name: Appointment Date To
A0139.description: Date that the appointment may be up to.  If the appointment needs to be on a particular date, this will be the same as the 'From date', if it can be any date from a specified date, this will be empty e.g. 20020308.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0020.name: Appointment Description
A0020.description: This is the description for the Appointment Qualifier Code.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0019.name: Appointment Qualifier Code
A0019.description: Code which defines the appointment qualifier. This can be related to the Appointment dates and it can be related to the Appointment times.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0140.name: Appointment Time From
A0140.description: The actual time, or start time of a range e.g. 090000 (9am) or AM (any time in the morning).  If this is blank then it can be any time from.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0141.name: Appointment Time To
A0141.description: The time to which this relates to, within a time range.  If this is blank then it can be any time to.  If both the from and to date are blank then it can be any time e.g. 160000 (4pm) or PM (anytime in the afternoon).

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0024.name: Asset Class Code
A0024.description: A code to indicate the classification of the Asset.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General
As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0158.name: Asset Location Notes
A0158.description: This is extra notes if the Location Code is insufficient.  It must be entered if the Location Code is 'Other'.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0037.name: Asset Status Code
A0037.description: Asset status code (Industry Standard), can also identify the condition of the Asset.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General
As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0173.name: Attribute Number
A0173.description: This is the value attributed to a data item, as defined in the RGMA.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0174.name: Attribute Value
A0174.description: This is the value for the data item identified, to be entered in the tables.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0006.name: Building Name Number
A0006.description: This is the Name and/or Number of the Building.  The Name is first. Building number in address (Industry Standard).  Premises within a building name and number range should be held as a Building Name in the form of 'Tower House School 14-22'.  Following PAF usage, this item will also take the value of any non-standard house or building number e.g. "12-13", or "6A".  Also holds the name of any sub-usage divided building such as a block of flats, when the name of an individual flat will be held in the Sub Building Name_Number.This can also be the plot number e.g Plot 58
A0006.notes: The definition of this has been taken from the current S70 Building Name and Building Number fields combined. It is equivalent to the SAF Metering Point address line 3.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0039.name: Care Category Code
A0039.description: A code used to define the type of special conditions a person may have. Leading zero's should be populated where required by the relevant Valid Set value.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0040.name: Care Category Description
A0040.description: Description of the type of special conditions a person may have.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0044.name: Collar Status Code
A0044.description: Code to identify the status of the collar. The absence of a collar where one was notified is used to alert the Meter Worker to potential tampering.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0045.name: Collar Status Description
A0045.description: Description associated with the collar status code.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0050.name: Contact Description
A0050.description: This is the description for the Mechanism Code.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0049.name: Contact Mechanism Code
A0049.description: The type of communication media that can be used (Industry Standard).

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0106.name: Contact Mechanism Value
A0106.description: The value related to the contact mechanism code.Examples:   020 7432 3005, SPAA@electralink.co.uk.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0053.name: Contract Reference
A0053.description: The contract reference the supplier has with the service provider, this is purely commercial.  This may indicate the Service Level Agreement / responses required.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0036.name: Conversion Basis Code
A0036.description: This is the conversion basis the converter is set up to convert against.  The code indicates if the converter compensates for e.g. 'T' Temperature.Amongst the wide range of converters produced one, some or all of the values identified within the C&D regs may be applied  pressure, temperature, compressibility and/or density. The cost of the models will vary according to this functionality. The majority of converters fitted at the current time convert for pressure, temperature and compressibility only. Compressibility and density conversion only applies above 2 bar metering pressure. Density conversion functionality tends to come with flow computers only and some of these models even take gas composition into account.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0074.name: Conversion Factor
A0074.description: A fixed factor applied where no Converter is fitted and the meter reading needs to be corrected for pressure, altitude and/or temperature.  This is a fixed value for domestic, provided by Ofgem (1.02264) but can vary for other component types (or Assets in the context of this document) at a Meter Point.  Fixed by GT according to location, height, pressure and temperature.
A0074.notes: This should be maintained even if there is a converter, to allow for conversion of readings from the meter should the converter malfunction.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0027.name: Converter Conversion Factor
A0027.description: A fixed factor applied where the Converter does not convert for all inputs, so readings still require correction to account for variations due to pressure, altitude and / or temperature.
A0027.notes: This has been included as a billing requirement (not audit) as an Asset may not have a task associated with it (e.g. install task type).

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0012.name: County
A0012.description: The county name for postal purposes.  For PAF validated addresses this is as specified by the Royal Mail within the Post code Address File e.g. West Midlands.  NOTE:  The Postal County may differ from the county within which the premise/land parcel is physically located.
A0012.notes: There is no equivalent field on the S70, it has been introduced to be in line with the SAF Metering Point Address Line 9.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0184.name: Created date
A0184.description: Date of creation of the input file.  Example 20020312.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.
Any within the constraints of format.

A0122.name: Cross-Ref Other External Job Reference
A0122.description: Used to identify another job, by a company other than the one to which this request applies.  If the external job or Organisation needs to be identified this will be provided in the Job Comments

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0058.name: Cross-Ref Other Internal Job Reference
A0058.description: Used to identify another job which relates to Organisation to which this request applies.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0178.name: Data Update Code
A0178.description: Identifies whether the record contains corrected data.  If it is blank then all the information contained in the record is new data or for reference purposes.

A0010.name: Dependent Locality

A0112.name: Measuring Capacity
A0112.description: The maximum volume of gas (Q Max) that can be put through an Asset per hour, based on the manufacturers maximum value.  This information is not on the Asset itself, but is published (the manufactures maximum value).  The service size will be one of the factors which determine the capacity of the Asset required. Permitted values for the relevant Product Id are defined in MDD.

A0121.name: Number of Dials or Digits
A0121.description: Number of significant dials or digits on the Asset which are considered during the Asset reading.  Used to validate Asset readings and to determine the number of complete units consumed.  For a converter this is the number of dials for the converted or unconverted register as indicated in the field above.  Where they are both the same, only the unconverted dials will be entered. Permitted values for the relevant Product Id are defined in MDD.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data Meter Product Table

A0149.name: OAMI Inspection Date
A0149.description: This is the date that the accuracy of the Register of a Meter or Converter is checked and will usually be done by a qualified installer e.g. 20020531.  If it is not present then (other than by commercial agreement) a separate job is required for an inspector to check it.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0163.name: Payment Method Code
A0163.description: The coded value describing the payment method of the product.It is only applicable for Measuring Assets.  This value is used with the status, for the relevant Measuring Assets to determine if billing of the Meter Point is credit or prepayment e.g. credit meter with a prepayment module which is active would be prepayment.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data Meter Product Table
As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0100.name: Person Notes
A0100.description: Free text information applicable to the customer.Examples: dog at premises, always shut the gate.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0107.name: Person Type Code
A0107.description: Code for the person contact type.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0108.name: Person Type Description
A0108.description: Description of the person contact type.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0013.name: Post Code
A0013.description: An abbreviated form of address provided in this format by the Royal Mail and used by them in identifying one or more delivery points e.g. a property or post box.  This contains a valid set of any alpha numeric set of characters where alpha characters are in the standard English language set.NOTE:  The Post Code is comprised of two halves, the Out Code and the In Code.  The Out Code defines the Royal Mail area (character 1-2) and district (character 3 and 4) in which the premises associated with the Post Code reside e.g. B65.The In Code e.g. 5GT is used to identify a small number of premises within the Street the Out Code identifies.  The Post Code must be used in conjunction with a building/Sub building name/number to uniquely identify it.The Incode and Outcode are always separated by a space.Example:  SW1  6NC
A0013.notes: The definition of this has been taken from a combination of the current S70 Post Code Out and In codes. The SAF Metering Point address Post Code contains this as the first part of the 'Post Code' field.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0011.name: Post Town
A0011.description: This is also known as 'Locality'.  It is the name of the town in which the address lies e.g. London.  NOTE:  The Post Town may differ from the town within which the premise/land parcel is physically located.
A0011.notes: The definition of this has been taken from the current S70. This is equivalent to the SAF Metering Point address line 8.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0008.name: Thoroughfare
A0008.description: The main street associated with an address.  NOTE: In certain circumstances  predominantly rural areas, there may not be any street named as part of the postal address e.g. High Street.
A0008.notes: The definition of this has been taken from the current S70 'Principle Street'. This is equivalent to the SAF Metering Point address line 5.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0088.name: Title
A0088.notes: Market Participants should be mindful that UK Link includes a defined set of allowable values for this data item.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0056.name: Transaction Comment
A0056.description: Free format comments on the job action taken/to be taken. This can include: Job instructions e.g. collect the meter, special tools they may need, if a bypass is to be fitted, Meter collection address, The job that the 'Cross-Ref Other Job Reference' field relates to would be indicated here e.g. service pipe. Examples:  Collect meter on the way to the address etc.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0189.name: Transaction Count
A0189.description: Count excludes the Header and Trailer.  For response transactions this is the count of RESP/REJFL's  (not TROUTs)

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0055.name: Transaction Reference
A0055.description: This reference is used to assist with reconciling responses to the original transactions sent and may be used for tracking purposes e.g. a notification of a job has been returned further to the request.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0057.name: Transaction Status Change Reason Code
A0057.description: A Code which qualifies the reason for the change of Work Status.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0062.name: Transaction Status Change Reason Description
A0062.description: The description of the Transaction Status Change Reason Code.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0142.name: Transaction Status Code
A0142.description: Code which defines what status the job is in.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0143.name: Transaction Status Description
A0143.description: The description of the 'Transaction Status Code'.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0144.name: Transaction Type Code
A0144.description: A Code which uniquely identifies the type of work.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0145.name: Transaction Type Description
A0145.description: Description of the meter work type.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0167.name: Transaction Type Reason Code
A0167.description: This is the reason for the work  in the case of Other Visits, this provides more information on the type of work required.   This can also assist in determining the cost of the visit.It is considered this field is likely to be defined commercially, but structured so it can be used to assist with charging.This may also indicate if there has been a variance to the job requested.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0151.name: Transaction Type Reason Description
A0151.description: Description of the meter work type.

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data General

A0018.name: Unique Property-Reference Number
A0018.description: A unique reference number identifying an individual address and its associated land parcel.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.

A0123.name: Units of Measure
A0123.description: Units in which the volume is expressed. At the time of writing legacy flows contain a 'Metric/Imperial' indicator.  This can be deduced from this field if it is cu M (M) or cu ft (I).

validSet.text: As defined within Market Domain Data Meter Product Table

A0021.name: Year of Manufacture
A0021.description: Year of manufacture for the asset as stamped on the asset e.g. 1999.  This is mandatory for certain assets e.g. Meters.

validSet.text: Any within the constraints of format.
